blockers: Clone-wrapper stdout is discarded instead of being redirected to stderr, so useful progress and failure diagnostics written there are hidden.
overall_reasoning: The agent correctly traced the contamination to the git clone subprocess in antidote-script, kept the production edit tightly scoped, and ran the focused and full repository-native test suites. However, redirecting clone stdout to /dev/null violates the explicit requirement to fix the generated-script leak without hiding useful diagnostics. The deterministic verifier confirmed both that redirected wrapper progress was not preserved on stderr and that failed-command diagnostics written to stdout disappeared. The added regression only asserts an empty stdout stream and suppresses stderr, so it does not exercise the required diagnostic-preservation behavior and allowed the central semantic defect to pass locally. No clarification was needed because the stream contract was explicit in the task.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The one-line redirection is easy to understand, and the regression setup is readable, although the test description overstates what it verifies.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The implementation uses the existing Zsh function and clitest Markdown conventions and introduces no unrelated dependencies or abstractions.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: The patch is proportional and limited to the clone stream plus focused coverage, despite a long and somewhat meandering investigation.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Sending subprocess stdout to /dev/null silently removes potentially useful wrapper progress and failure diagnostics, contrary to the required observable behavior.
rating_rationales.test_quality: The agent ran the focused 86-test file and all 684 tests, but the new regression checks only that stdout is empty; by redirecting stderr away, it cannot detect the required preservation of wrapper diagnostics.

strengths: Traced the generated-script path and identified the exact clone subprocess contaminating stdout.
Kept the production change and regression coverage narrowly scoped.
Ran both the focused clitest file and the full 684-test suite.
summary: Focused and well-investigated stream-isolation attempt, but not mergeable because it discards useful clone diagnostics instead of preserving them on stderr.
